learning.whatWillYouLearn: Prerequisites: Arrays, Two Pointers, Loops, Swapping, Time Complexity
learning.whyDoesThisExist: Imagine arranging books on a shelf where empty spaces (0's) should be moved to the end while keeping the order of all actual books unchanged.
learning.realWorldAnalogy: Imagine arranging books on a shelf where empty spaces (0's) should be moved to the end while keeping the order of all actual books unchanged.
learning.whenToUseThis: Use the Stable Two Pointer pattern whenever elements matching a condition must be shifted while preserving the order of the remaining elements.
learning.signals: Whenever elements satisfying a condition must be moved while preserving their order, think of the Stable Two Pointer pattern.
learning.expectedLearningOutcome: I will know when to use this pattern.

problem.problemStatement: Given an integer array nums, move all 0's to the end while maintaining the relative order of the non-zero elements. Perform the operation in-place without making a copy of the array.

examples.explanation: All non-zero elements remain in the same order while zeroes move to the end.
The array already satisfies the condition.

thinkingProcess.naiveIdea: Store all non-zero elements in a temporary array, then append all zeroes and copy everything back.
thinkingProcess.keyInsight: Maintain one pointer indicating the position where the next non-zero element should be placed. Traverse the array, and whenever a non-zero element is found, swap it into its correct position.
thinkingProcess.ahaMoment: Every non-zero element should be moved to the earliest available position.
thinkingProcess.patternRecognition: Whenever elements satisfying a condition must be moved while preserving their order, think of the Stable Two Pointer pattern.
thinkingProcess.whyItWorks: Each element is visited exactly once, and swaps happen only when necessary. The relative order of non-zero elements is preserved while using constant extra space.
thinkingProcess.howToRecognizeSimilar: Whenever elements satisfying a condition must be moved while preserving their order, think of the Stable Two Pointer pattern.
commonWrongPaths: Not preserving the order of non-zero elements.
Using an extra array despite the in-place requirement.
Incrementing the insertion pointer for zeroes.

commonMisconceptions: Not preserving the order of non-zero elements.
Using an extra array despite the in-place requirement.
Incrementing the insertion pointer for zeroes.

pattern.name: Two Pointers (Stable Partition)
pattern.recognitionSignals: Whenever elements satisfying a condition must be moved while preserving their order, think of the Stable Two Pointer pattern.

approaches.title: Use Extra Array
approaches.idea: Store all non-zero elements in a temporary array, then append all zeroes and copy everything back.

approaches.title: Overwrite then Fill Zeroes
approaches.idea: Overwrite the array with all non-zero elements first, then fill the remaining positions with zeroes.
algorithm: Fill the remaining positions with zeroes.
approaches.dryRun: Compact non-zero elements, then append zeroes.

approaches.title: Two Pointers (Stable Partition)
approaches.idea: Maintain two pointers. One pointer scans the array while the other marks the position where the next non-zero element belongs. Whenever a non-zero element is encountered, swap it with the insertion pointer and advance the insertion pointer.
approaches.intuition: Each element is visited exactly once, and swaps happen only when necessary. The relative order of non-zero elements is preserved while using constant extra space.

visualization.description: Move every non-zero element to its correct position.
steps: Zeroes automatically shift to the end.

interviewFollowUps: Can this be solved without swapping?
How many swaps occur in the worst case?
Can you move non-zeroes to the end instead?
How would you partition based on another value?
interviewTips: Explain why the insertion pointer works.
State O(n) time and O(1) space.

cheatSheet.keyObservation: Every non-zero element should be moved to the earliest available position.
cheatSheet.algorithm: Scan → Swap Non-zero → Advance Insert Pointer.

commonMistakes: Not preserving the order of non-zero elements.
Using an extra array despite the in-place requirement.
Incrementing the insertion pointer for zeroes.
